title: Aleuritopteris tamburii
taxonomy: Aleuritopteris tamburii is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger group of vascular plants known as the Pteridophyta. As a member of the Pteridophyta, Aleuritopteris tamburii is also classified as a type of cryptogam. The specific classification of the plant is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Pteridaceae, Genus: Aleuritopteris, Species: A. tamburii. The exact classification may vary depending on the source.

intro: Aleuritopteris tamburii, also known as the tamburi fern, is a type of plant native to the Himalayan region that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, a family of vascular plants commonly known as ferns. The tamburi fern has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aleuritopteris tamburii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the tamburi fern is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
